macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin holds near $62K–$63K as traders watch key support
items.summary: Bitcoin was trading in a narrow range around $62,200 to $63,200, with some market coverage describing it as consolidating rather than making a decisive breakout. CryptoSlate also showed Bitcoin up about 2.35% over 24 hours at roughly $63,272, indicating a modest positive move overall.

items.title: Ethereum edges higher alongside broader crypto market strength
items.summary: Ethereum was up about 2.43% over 24 hours to roughly $1,670, moving in step with Bitcoin’s modest gain. The available market snapshot suggests a generally firmer tone across major crypto assets rather than a sharp sector-wide rally.

items.title: Bitcoin volatility becomes the focus of new ETF income strategies
items.summary: CryptoSlate highlighted a report that BlackRock is moving to turn Bitcoin volatility into ETF income, underscoring continued institutional product development around crypto exposure. This is notable as a sign of ongoing Wall Street experimentation with Bitcoin-linked yield products.

items.title: Japan’s crypto reform bill moves closer, with potential tax and market implications
items.summary: Reporting in the daily crypto news roundup said Japan’s crypto reform bill is edging closer, and another circulating item said Japan has reclassified crypto as financial products while cutting tax rates and opening the door to spot crypto ETFs. The specific policy details in the second account should be treated cautiously because they were not confirmed by the more direct news result.

items.title: U.S. CFTC signals possible green light for sports and election prediction markets
items.summary: Crypto News Australia reported that the CFTC issued a rule proposal signaling support for sports and election prediction markets, a development relevant to blockchain-based prediction platforms and crypto-native trading venues. This is a regulatory story with potential spillover effects for tokenized market infrastructure.

items.title: Secret Service-led crypto seizure highlights ongoing enforcement pressure
items.summary: The U.S. Secret Service said it led an international operation that seized more than $225 million in cryptocurrency tied to a money laundering network and arrested two suspects. The action reinforces that enforcement against crypto-enabled illicit finance remains active and coordinated across jurisdictions.

items.title: Ripple’s Garlinghouse criticizes Dimon amid renewed U.S. crypto policy debate
items.summary: Crypto News Australia reported that Ripple CEO Brad Garlinghouse called out Jamie Dimon over criticism of the CLARITY Act, keeping U.S. crypto legislation in the spotlight. The item reflects continuing industry lobbying around clearer digital asset rules.

items.title: Humanity Protocol hack drains more than $36 million
items.summary: Crypto News Australia reported that Humanity Protocol lost over $36 million in H tokens after a hacker exploited bridge controls via a compromised laptop. This is one of the most significant project-level security incidents in the latest reporting window.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.
